evidence: 'relevant' appears as selection qualifier in three distinct sentences across the skill body

evidence: Lines 93-98: cp and shortcuts sign write to $OUTPUT_DIR path derived from env var; path validation limited to path-separator check on --name
description: Script writes signed .shortcut and XML archive to user-configured output directory outside the repo
fp_reason: Writing to a user-configured output_dir is the plugin's explicit and documented purpose; the only write target is the shortcut output directory, not arbitrary paths
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish intentional plugin-output writes (declared in plugin manifest userConfig) from uncontrolled writes to arbitrary filesystem paths

evidence: codex SKILL.md frontmatter lacks 'effort: max' and 'allowed-tools:' present in claude SKILL.md frontmatter
description: Codex SKILL.md frontmatter omits effort and allowed-tools fields declared in the Claude mirror; divergence appears intentional for platform compatibility
fp_reason: Codex does not use the same plugin frontmatter schema as Claude Code; the Codex skill body explicitly documents Codex-specific script invocation patterns
rule_gap: Rule should allow sibling-skill divergence when the skill explicitly documents its target platform's different invocation model
